passage.title: Class Job Wheel
passage.text: Our group keeps a plan chart by the door. During homeroom, Evan checks it before music class. The plan chart has small cards for helper jobs and class turns for the day's job. The class leader asks us to move the right card and say our plan. Sometimes we compare today's label with yesterday's label and notice one small change. We like the plan chart because it helps us start class in a calm way.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the plan chart is used.
The first sentence says the plan chart is by the door.

questions.prompt: When does Evan use the plan chart?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Evan uses it during homeroom.

questions.prompt: What does the plan chart have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the plan chart has helper cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
